title: Programmers Notes
wikitext:
[]: Programmers Notes             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Aug 02, 2005 8:44 am One of the better aspects of the Platinum edition is that a certain number of the Japanese captions and writing on screen are included in the subtitles on screen. This is quite useful and illuminating at times. However I never expected these subtitles to provoke the kind of coffee upsetting spasms induced by the following. [URL=http://imageshack.us] [/URL] Droll to say the least. But I can't help but wonder if the translation might be taking liberties. Did Naoko really write this. Submitted for your consideration.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Aug 02, 2005 9:02 am Yeah, I saw this and considered posting it. I found it incredibly funny.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 02, 2005 12:13 pm there were multiple programmers right? so it is possible that it was someone else whom gendo had pissed off... there is, however, a chance that it was naoko...so your theory is plausible

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Tue Aug 09, 2005 3:34 am Makes me want to get the Platinum edition so much now. Just we you think you have seen it all, it just comes out with more suprises

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Aug 09, 2005 9:46 am But the prices make me want to cry...

[]:             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Thu Aug 11, 2005 12:13 pm Oh stop crying.... though volumes 1-4 cost me like $125... XD anything for my fav show right!?! RIGHT!?! Anyways I thought the Ikari you jerk thing was pretty cool. It was probly some sort of in the moment act but it could also have been a warning to her daughter like "dont fuck this guy, I did now look where i am" lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Aug 11, 2005 2:31 pm The blown-up screencap is unavailable. I don't know the kanji for Ikari, but I definitely see "baka" in there. It appears to read "Ikari no BAKA YAROU" or "Idiot rascal Ikari". Beneath that, it looks like the katakana "me", but it could just be an "X" drawn on the note buried underneath. Being that Naoko was fucking Gendo up until her demise, I'm not certain that she would write that. It is possible that she was intensely angered by his endless fixation on his wife, and this was written in emotional exasperation as the affair began to grow real emotional roots. It could also be addressing Yui and not Gendo, assuming "Ikari" is what the kanji reads. She was the original one, after all.

[]:             Quote Post by RuaDragon » Fri Aug 12, 2005 8:08 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: The blown-up screencap is unavailable. I don't know the kanji for Ikari, but I definitely see "baka" in there. It appears to read "Ikari no BAKA YAROU" or "Idiot rascal Ikari".

It appears to read "Ikari no BAKA YAROU" or "Idiot rascal Ikari". Beneath that, it looks like the katakana "me", but it could just be an "X" drawn on the note buried underneath. Being that Naoko was fucking Gendo up until her demise, I'm not certain that she would write that. It is possible that she was intensely angered by his endless fixation on his wife, and this was written in emotional exasperation as the affair began to grow real emotional roots. It could also be addressing Yui and not Gendo, assuming "Ikari" is what the kanji reads. She was the original one, after all.
